verses.1: Give thanks to the LORD, call upon His name; Make His deeds known among the peoples.
verses.2: Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; Tell of all His wonders.
verses.3: Boast in His holy name; May the heart of those who seek the LORD be joyful.
verses.4: Seek the LORD and His strength; Seek His face continually.
verses.5: Remember His wonders which He has done, His marvels and the judgments spoken by His mouth,
verses.6: You descendants of Abraham, His servant, You sons of Jacob, His chosen ones!
verses.7: He is the LORD our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
verses.8: He has remembered His covenant forever, The word which He commanded to a thousand generations,
verses.9: The covenant which He made with Abraham, And His oath to Isaac.
verses.10: Then He confirmed it to Jacob as a statute, To Israel as an everlasting covenant,
verses.11: Saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan As the portion of your inheritance,”
verses.12: When they were only a few people in number, Very few, and strangers in it.
verses.13: And they wandered from nation to nation, From one kingdom to another people,
verses.14: He allowed no one to oppress them, And He rebuked kings for their sakes, saying,
verses.15: “Do not touch My anointed ones, And do not harm My prophets.”
verses.16: And He called for a famine upon the land; He broke the whole staff of bread.
verses.17: He sent a man before them, Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
verses.18: They forced his feet into shackles, He was put in irons;
verses.19: Until the time that his word came to pass, The word of the LORD refined him.
verses.20: The king sent and released him, The ruler of peoples, and set him free.
verses.21: He made him lord of his house, And ruler over all his possessions,
verses.22: To imprison his high officials at will, That he might teach his elders wisdom.
verses.23: Israel also came into Egypt; So Jacob lived in the land of Ham.
verses.24: And He made His people very fruitful, And made them stronger than their enemies.
verses.25: He turned their heart to hate His people, To deal cunningly with His servants.
verses.26: He sent His servant Moses, And Aaron, whom He had chosen.
verses.27: They performed His wondrous acts among them, And miracles in the land of Ham.
verses.28: He sent darkness and made it dark; And they did not rebel against His words.
verses.29: He turned their waters into blood, And caused their fish to die.
verses.30: Their land swarmed with frogs Even in the chambers of their kings.
verses.31: He spoke, and a swarm of flies And gnats invaded all their territory.
verses.32: He gave them hail for rain, And flaming fire in their land.
verses.33: He also struck their vines and their fig trees, And smashed the trees of their territory.
verses.34: He spoke, and locusts came, And creeping locusts, beyond number,
verses.35: And they ate all the vegetation in their land, And ate the fruit of their ground.
verses.36: He also fatally struck all the firstborn in their land, The first fruits of all their vigor.
verses.37: Then He brought the Israelites out with silver and gold, And among His tribes there was not one who stumbled.
verses.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, For the dread of them had fallen upon the Egyptians.
verses.39: He spread out a cloud as a covering, And fire to illumine by night.
verses.40: They asked, and He brought quail, And satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
verses.41: He opened the rock and water flowed out; It ran in the dry places like a river.
verses.42: For He remembered His holy word With His servant Abraham;
verses.43: And He led out His people with joy, His chosen ones with a joyful shout.
verses.44: He also gave them the lands of the nations, So that they might take possession of the fruit of the peoples’ labor,
verses.45: And that they might keep His statutes And comply with His laws; Praise the LORD!
